summary: Evidence backing and confidence caveats for State of the Web vision audits.

be-inclusive.notes: Axe blocked/failed is not an issue; it is pass with low confidence unless other probes find a problem.

respect-user-preferences.notes: Original batches used colorScheme and animation probes; reduced-motion video was missing and is being added by supplemental pass.

be-fast-and-stable.notes: Original scale batches had CLS/layout but not Lighthouse/trace; confidence caveated until supplemental evidence lands.

be-discoverable.notes: Crawler/no-JS coverage and meta/title/h1 signals were parsed directly.

be-agent-ready.notes: Judged from no-JS/agent-visible content and semantic/metadata probes.

be-sustainable.notes: Judged from transferred bytes and request counts. Raw HAR retained but summary used for reasoning.

be-private-and-secure.notes: Judged from HTTPS/HSTS, third-party request surface, script hosts, and headers where available.

be-internationalised.notes: Basic html lang/dir checks only; deeper locale formatting flows not tested.

follow-best-practices.notes: Original batches used metadata/viewport/probe evidence; Lighthouse best-practices being added by supplemental pass.

adapt-to-the-form-factor.notes: Judged from mobile/desktop layout metrics and horizontal overflow.

support-core-task-success.notes: Homepage primary task availability only; full task completion flows not exercised.

be-memory-efficient.notes: Baseline heap alone is not a pass. Same-session forced-GC memory audit required.

be-trustworthy.notes: Passive homepage evidence is not treated as a pass; needs consent/account/commerce/dark-pattern flow review.

be-resilient.notes: Manifest/SW presence is not treated as a pass; needs no-JS/offline/reload fallback testing.

maximize-content-reduce-noise.notes: Judged mostly from request/script surface and page chrome signals; visual noise not manually reviewed for every site.
